steps.title: Identify the Goal's Requirement
steps.content: The student wants to “emphasize a difference”—this requires explicit comparison language, not just facts.

steps.title: Apply to the Choices
steps.content:
B accomplishes this with “Unlike traditional biennials,” directly establishing the contrast before stating the key differences: different venues and an uneven schedule. Choices A, C, and D either present isolated facts without contrasting (A), define the term through negation (C), or obscure the distinction (D).
That matches choice B — B is correct.
